comment: Initial benchmark baselines captured on the maintainer's machine (gcc-15.2 release build, Linux x86_64). Future runs compare against these; +/- 20 percent is the soft regression band, anything beyond triggers a manual investigation.

benchmarks.name: info / hello-hardened
benchmarks.notes: LIEF parse + mitigations + dynamic-import scan dominates the cost.

benchmarks.name: disasm x86_64 / 48 bytes
benchmarks.notes: cs_open + cs_disasm + cs_close per call; thread-local handle caching would cut this further.

benchmarks.name: gadget / hello-hardened / depth=4
benchmarks.notes: Forward-decode at every byte offset; sequential. Parallel variant is a deferred optimization.

benchmarks.name: find_gadgets / 4 KiB synthetic
benchmarks.notes: Synthetic stress: 2048 identical pop-rdi-ret pairs. Dedup keeps the output small.

benchmarks.name: libc id / 2 offsets
benchmarks.notes: In-binary fingerprint table walk; constant time per (technique, era) check.

benchmarks.name: libc offsets / glibc 2.34
benchmarks.notes: Same in-binary table, filter by id.
